pr.title: Split-up improvements across common, GM/MN, TY, and baselib
pr.author: fjooord

summary: PR #2636 made small split-up matching improvements across camera, common fighter bury code, GM/MN menu code, TY display, map collision, and sysdolphin/baselib. The PR body and bot regression report claim 5 new exact matches, 8 unmatched-item improvements, +1608 matched code bytes, +24 matched data bytes, and no broken, fuzzy, or metric regressions.

key_files.role: Camera_80030CFC exact-match stack/local layout adjustment: added explicit 12-byte local pad and reduced PAD_STACK.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: listed as new exact match

key_files.role: Added tightly scoped force_active .sdata2 literals and removed dummy 8-byte local from ftCo_800C0D0C; PR claims .sdata2 and ftCo_800C0D0C exact matches.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: force_active literals ftCo_804D8C28/30/38/3C and ftCo_800C0D0C hunk
human_pr_text: reviewer notes on split-owned .sdata2 range and new exact matches

key_files.role: Added PAD_STACK in fn_8018325C and used local int pointer indexing into lbl_80473700 for fn_801884F8 exact match.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: reviewer note says pointer indexing mirrors nearby fn_8018846C; fn_801884F8 listed as new exact match

key_files.role: Reshaped mnCount_8025092C stack/local scope with an added PAD_STACK and inner block around CountEntry locals; bot report lists it as an unmatched-item improvement.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists mnCount_8025092C improvement 99.92% to 99.94%

key_files.role: Changed mpCheckFloor distance calculation to reuse a loop-scoped dist2 local and SQ macro expressions, with a small pad local.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists mpCheckFloor improvement 99.68% to 99.88%

key_files.role: Adjusted un_8031BC54 declaration order and category/archive indexing locals; PR claims exact match and notes earlier string-base/assert reshaping was dropped.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: tdisplay string literals/HSD_ASSERT note and un_8031BC54 exact match

key_files.role: Changed AXDriver_8038BF6C intermediate float expression ordering/products; bot report lists unmatched-item improvement.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists AXDriver_8038BF6C improvement 99.62% to 99.84%

key_files.role: Changed hsd_80393DA0 memset size to exclude x24 field before setting buffer fields; bot report lists unmatched-item improvement.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists hsd_80393DA0 improvement 99.78% to 99.81%

what_changed: Added or rearranged stack padding and local declarations in Camera_80030CFC, fn_8018325C, mnCount_8025092C, mnItemSw_8023453C, mn_8022B3A0, fn_8017F2A4, and gm_801AC6D8_OnEnter.

Added force_active .sdata2 constants ftCo_804D8C28, ftCo_804D8C30, ftCo_804D8C38, and ftCo_804D8C3C in ftCo_Bury.c, with PR body stating they correspond to existing symbols.txt entries and the split-owned .sdata2 range.
Changed fn_801884F8 from struct-field result_cache accesses to int pointer indexing through &lbl_80473700 at indices 65 and 66.
Changed mpCheckFloor to compute squared distance with SQ(px_sp54 - ax) + SQ(py_sp50 - ay) and a reused dist2 local instead of separate dx/dy/dx2/dy2 temporaries.
Changed tdisplay un_8031BC54 to keep separate c2 and cat locals so archive indexing uses c2 while matanim category can be remapped when the signed category is -1.
Changed AXDriver_8038BF6C float product expressions around left/right sqrt and inverse-sqrt intermediates.
Changed hsd_80393DA0 to memset hsd_804CF7E8 only through sizeof(hsd_804CF7E8) - sizeof(hsd_804CF7E8.x24), then set out_buf and buf_size.

smart_moves.text: The PR explicitly dropped an earlier tdisplay string-base/assert reshaping and kept source string literals plus HSD_ASSERT form, avoiding data/string churn in a matching PR.
The ftCo_Bury .sdata2 literals were scoped with force_active on/reset and justified in the PR body as matching existing symbols.txt entries and the ftCo_Bury.c split-owned .sdata2 range.
The author documented that gm_1832 pointer indexing was a local codegen tactic mirrored from a nearby function, instead of presenting it as a broad style preference.
Stack/local-shape tactics were tied to a regression report with exact-match and no-regression claims.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Reviewer Notes fourth bullet and Verification section

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a matching PR touches ordinary string literals or assert forms, prefer leaving literals and existing HSD_ASSERT-style source intact unless the PR has explicit data-ownership evidence for reshaping them.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: earlier tdisplay string-base/assert reshaping was dropped; source string literals and HSD_ASSERT kept

decomp_lessons.lesson: For local stack/codegen closure, declaration order, explicit small pad locals, inner block scoping, and narrow PAD_STACK changes can be useful tactics, but the PR evidence frames them as narrow and regression-backed rather than general style.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Camera_80030CFC, mnCount_8025092C, mnItemSw_8023453C, mn_8022B3A0, gmregclear, gmstaffroll, mplib hunks
human_pr_text: stack/local-shape tweaks kept narrow and backed by regression report

decomp_lessons.lesson: When local codegen requires non-struct indexing into a global object, cite nearby matched pointer-style access as local evidence and keep the tactic localized to the affected function.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_801884F8 changed result_cache fields to ptr[65]/ptr[66]
human_pr_text: pointer indexing mirrors nearby fn_8018846C

decomp_lessons.lesson: Project macros can improve both readability and codegen when they express the operation: mpCheckFloor replaced expanded squared-distance temporaries with SQ macro expressions and improved the unmatched item.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mpCheckFloor SQ(px_sp54 - ax) + SQ(py_sp50 - ay)
text_corpus: bot report mpCheckFloor improved 99.68% to 99.88%

naming_conventions.convention: Address-style names such as fn_801884F8, mn_8022B3A0, hsd_80393DA0, and ftCo_804D8C28 were retained; this PR did not attempt speculative semantic renames.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: changed symbols retain address-style names
human_pr_text: match/improvement list uses existing address-style names

naming_conventions.convention: Short role locals remain common where roles are obvious, such as gobj, cobj, jobj, fp, src, top, data, tbl, cat, and id.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mnmain, gmstaffroll, ftCo_Bury, tdisplay hunks

naming_conventions.convention: Temporary matching locals may use suffixes or stack-style names when needed for codegen, as seen with arg1_, arg2_, c2, sp44, sp3C, and _PAD, but the PR evidence supports these only as local matching tactics.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mnitemsw arg1_/arg2_, tdisplay c2, camera _PAD

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use explicit pad locals plus reduced or added PAD_STACK to tune stack layout for exact or near matches.
examples: Camera_80030CFC added u8 _PAD[12] and changed PAD_STACK(14) to PAD_STACK(2)
mnCount_8025092C added PAD_STACK(4) and inner block scoping

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use local block scope to constrain lifetime and stack layout of large arrays and temporaries.
examples: mnCount_8025092C wraps CountEntry entries, tmp, and loop locals in an inner block after PAD_STACK(4)

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use pointer indexing as a narrow codegen tactic when nearby local code supports that access shape.
examples: fn_801884F8 uses int* ptr = (int*) &lbl_80473700 and ptr[65]/ptr[66] instead of lbl_80473700.result_cache fields
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: pointer indexing mirrors nearby fn_8018846C

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Prefer canonical expression macros when they match the operation and improve codegen.
examples: mpCheckFloor uses SQ(px_sp54 - ax) + SQ(py_sp50 - ay) instead of separate dx/dy/dx2/dy2 locals

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: For split-owned data literals, force_active can be tightly scoped around the specific constants when symbol and split ownership evidence is documented.
examples: ftCo_Bury.c force_active on/reset around four ftCo_804D8Cxx .sdata2 constants
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: constants correspond to symbols.txt entries and ftCo_Bury.c split-owned .sdata2 range

review_feedback.text: Earlier tdisplay string-base/assert reshaping for un_8031B1FC was dropped; this PR keeps source string literals and HSD_ASSERT form.
ftCo_Bury force_active .sdata2 literals are described as tightly scoped and corresponding to existing symbols.txt entries plus the ftCo_Bury.c split-owned .sdata2 range.
gm_1832 pointer indexing for fn_801884F8 is described as matching codegen and mirroring nearby pointer-style access in fn_8018846C.
No loaded review comments or formal reviews were present in this PR slice; review feedback is limited to PR body reviewer notes and the bot regression report.

follow_up_queries: Find PRs that dropped string-literal-to-symbol or assert reshaping during tdisplay matching.
Search gm_1832 for fn_8018846C and fn_801884F8 pointer-style access patterns around lbl_80473700.
Search ftCo_Bury .sdata2 symbols ftCo_804D8C28 ftCo_804D8C30 ftCo_804D8C38 ftCo_804D8C3C and split ownership evidence.
Find other exact-match PRs where inner block scoping of large local arrays changed MWCC stack layout.
Search mpCheckFloor and other map collision functions for SQ macro substitutions replacing expanded dx/dy squared-distance temporaries.
Find PAD_STACK adjustments tied to exact matches in camera, MN, GM, and baselib files.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR identity, state, counts, changed files
human_pr_text: PR summary, reviewer notes, verification claims
raw_diff: complete diff excerpt for 12 files
notes: The PR slice includes full changed-file metadata, full raw diff, PR body, and bot regression report.
No human review comments are loaded; reviewer intent cannot be inferred beyond the author's Reviewer Notes.
Verification claims are from the PR body and decomp-dev bot report; no independent build or objdiff was run during intake.
The statement that ftCo_Bury .sdata2 constants correspond to symbols.txt entries and split-owned range is PR-body evidence, not independently revalidated here.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Avoid string/assert reshaping without data ownership evidence
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2636, an earlier tdisplay string-base/assert reshaping for un_8031B1FC was dropped; the landed PR kept source string literals and HSD_ASSERT form while still achieving other matching improvements. This supports preserving ordinary literals/assert macros during code matching unless explicit data-ownership evidence justifies the change.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep stack/local-shape tactics narrow and regression-backed
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2636 used declaration order, explicit pad locals, inner block scoping, and PAD_STACK changes across several functions. The PR body states these stack/local-shape tweaks were narrow and backed by regression results, and the bot report recorded 5 new matches, 8 improvements, and no regressions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: camera.c, mncount.c, mnitemsw.c, mnmain.c, gmregclear.c, gmstaffroll.c, mplib.c

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use nearby local style to justify pointer indexing tactics
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2636 changed fn_801884F8 to use int pointer indexing into lbl_80473700. The PR body says this was to match codegen and mirrored nearby pointer-style access in fn_8018846C, supporting localized use of pointer indexing when nearby matched code provides evidence.

source_update_candidates.title: ftCo_Bury.c owns specific .sdata2 constants used in PR #2636
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2636 states that ftCo_Bury added tightly scoped force_active .sdata2 literals ftCo_804D8C28, ftCo_804D8C30, ftCo_804D8C38, and ftCo_804D8C3C, corresponding to existing symbols.txt entries and the ftCo_Bury.c split-owned .sdata2 range. Curator should verify symbols/split ownership before promoting as a path fact.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a source/path ownership claim from the PR body and should be verified against symbols and splits before becoming a path fact.

source_update_candidates.title: String literal/assert cleanup should be considered before data reshaping in matching PRs
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2636 provides historical evidence for keeping source string literals and HSD_ASSERT form after dropping earlier tdisplay string-base/assert reshaping. This may reinforce existing standards around no string-literal symbol regression, assert-report macros, and text-before-data matching.
source_update_candidates.reason: The existing loaded standards already cover this pattern; curator may want to add PR #2636 as supporting historical evidence rather than a new rule.

source_update_candidates.title: Force_active for data literals requires tight scope and ownership evidence
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2636 used a force_active on/reset block only around four ftCo_Bury .sdata2 literals and documented symbols.txt plus split-range ownership evidence. This could be cited as an example for tightly scoped pragma exceptions when data ownership is verified.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is compatible with existing standards but should not be promoted without curator verification of the symbol/split evidence.

rejection_notes: Do not generalize fn_801884F8 ptr[65]/ptr[66] into a preference for raw pointer indexing over typed fields; the PR frames it as local codegen matching evidence only.

Do not promote PAD_STACK, dummy pad locals, or declaration-order churn as accepted style without narrow regression evidence; this PR says the tweaks were backed by its report, not that they are broadly preferred.
Do not infer that force_active pragmas are generally acceptable for matching; this PR's evidence is limited to a tight ftCo_Bury .sdata2 block with claimed symbol and split ownership.
Do not claim human reviewer approval details beyond merge state; the loaded slice has no human review comments.
